name: Óðinn

sot_text.original: Óðinn er æðstr ok ellztr Ásanna. Hann ræðr öllum hlutum, ok svá sem önnur goðin eru máttug, þá þjóna honum öll svá sem börn föður.

translations.faulkes_1987: Odin is the highest and oldest of the Aesir. He rules all things, and though the other gods are powerful, they all serve him as children do a father.
translations.byock_2005: Odin is the highest and oldest of the Aesir. He rules over all things, and though the other gods are powerful, they all serve him as children serve a father.
translations.brodeur_1916: Odin is highest and oldest of the Æsir: he rules all things, and mighty as are the other gods, they all serve him as children obey a father.

historical_offerings: Wine, meat, weapons (votive deposits), and poetry.
common_invocations: All-Father, Lord of the Hanged, The Yule-God.
practical_application: Anchoring an oath, seeking wisdom through trial, and high-level strategy.
academic_note: Odin is the highest and oldest of the Aesir. His modern association with encryption stems from his 'winning' of the runes—the original code of the universe.

name: Þórr

sot_text.original: Sá er kallaðr Þórr eða Ása-Þórr. Hann er fremstr þeira, sá er kallaðr er Áss ok hergi hvílir. Hann er sterkastr allra goðanna ok manna.

translations.faulkes_1987: He is called Thor or Asa-Thor. He is the foremost of them, the one who is called Aesir's Thor and the strongest of all gods and men.
translations.byock_2005: He is called Thor or Asa-Thor. He is the foremost of them, he who is called the Aesir's Thor. He is the strongest of all the gods and the men.
translations.brodeur_1916: The foremost of them is Thor, he who is called Thor of the Æsir, or Thunder-Thor; he is the strongest of all the gods and the men.

historical_offerings: Stout ale, iron amulets, and hearty grain.
common_invocations: Protector of Midgard, Thunder-Thor, Friend of Humans.
practical_application: Active defense, boundary protection, and physical labor.
academic_note: Thor is defined as 'fremstr' (foremost) in strength. He is the primary protector of Midgard and the common man.

name: Baldr

sot_text.original: Annarr sonr Óðins er Baldr, ok er frá honum gott at segja. Hann er beztr ok hann lofa allir.

translations.faulkes_1987: Odin's second son is Baldr, and there is good to be told of him. He is the best, and all praise him.
translations.byock_2005: Odin's second son is Baldr, and there are good things to be said about him. He is the best, and everyone praises him.
translations.brodeur_1916: The second son of Odin is Baldr, and good things are to be said of him. He is best, and all praise him.

historical_offerings: White flowers, light-colored ale, and acts of mediation.
common_invocations: The Shining One, Best of the Aesir.
practical_application: Conflict resolution, seeking internal clarity, and acts of beauty.
academic_note: Baldr represents the ideal of beauty and wisdom. His death is the catalyst for the events leading to Ragnarök.

name: Týr

sot_text.original: Enn er sá Áss er Týr heitir. Hann er djarfastr ok bezt hugaðr, ok hann ræðr mjök sigri í orrostum.

translations.faulkes_1987: There is also an Aesir called Tyr. He is the most daring and stout-hearted, and he has a large share in victory in battles.
translations.byock_2005: There is also an Aesir named Tyr. He is the most daring and the most courageous, and he has a great deal of power over victory in battle.
translations.brodeur_1916: There is yet an Æsir, styled Týr: he is most daring, and best in stoutness of heart, and he has much sway over victory in battle.

historical_offerings: Legal documents (symbolic), iron, and hard-won personal trophies.
common_invocations: The One-Handed, God of the Thing.
practical_application: Professional ethics, making necessary sacrifices for community safety, and legal order.

modern_extrapolation.tech_sphere: Secure Authentication and Proof of Work

academic_note: The most daring and stout-hearted. He has much sway over victory in battle and legal order.

name: Heimdallr

sot_text.original: Heimdallr heitir einn. Hann er kallaðr hvíti Áss. Hann er mikill ok heilagr.

translations.faulkes_1987: One is called Heimdall. He is called the white Aesir. He is great and holy.
translations.byock_2005: Heimdall is the name of one. He is called the white Aesir. He is great and holy.
translations.brodeur_1916: Heimdallr is the name of one: he is called the White God. He is great and holy.

historical_offerings: Keys, horns, and objects associated with thresholds/guarding.
common_invocations: The White God, Watchman of Asgard.
practical_application: System monitoring, establishing security perimeters, and maintaining communal boundaries.
academic_note: The 'White God' and watchman of Asgard. He has extraordinary senses, hearing grass grow.

name: Loki

sot_text.original: Sá er enn taldr með Ásum, er sumir kalla rógbera Ásanna ok frumkveða flærðanna ok vömm allra goða ok manna.

translations.faulkes_1987: There is also one counted among the Aesir whom some call the slanderer of the Aesir and the source of deceit and the disgrace of all gods and men.
translations.byock_2005: There is also one counted among the Aesir whom some call the slanderer of the Aesir and the originator of deceits and the shame of all gods and men.
translations.brodeur_1916: Also numbered among the Æsir is he whom some call the backbiter of the Æsir, and the first father of falsehoods, and the blemish of all gods and men.

historical_offerings: Objects associated with fire or trickery.
common_invocations: The Slanderer, The Shape-shifter.
practical_application: Bug hunting, disruptive innovation, and identified critical flaws in a system.
academic_note: Counted among the Aesir but acts as their slanderer and source of deceit.

name: Frigg

sot_text.original: Frigg er æðst. Hon á þann bœ er Fensalir heita ok er hann mjök prúðligr.

translations.faulkes_1987: Frigg is the highest. She has a dwelling called Fensalir and it is very magnificent.
translations.byock_2005: Frigg is the foremost. She owns the hall called Fensalir and it is very splendid.
translations.brodeur_1916: Frigg is the first: she possesses the lordly abode which is called Fensalir.

historical_offerings: Linen, spinning tools, and keys.
common_invocations: Queen of Asgard, Foremost of Goddesses.
practical_application: Domestic infrastructure management, high-level diplomacy, and private prophecy.
academic_note: The highest goddess. She owns the magnificent hall Fensalir.

name: Eir

sot_text.original: Þriðja er Eir. Hon er læknir beztr.

translations.faulkes_1987: The third is Eir. She is the best physician.
translations.byock_2005: The third is Eir. She is the best of healers.
translations.brodeur_1916: The third is Eir: she is an extremely good physician.

historical_offerings: Healing herbs, water, and acts of nursing.
common_invocations: The Best Physician, Healer of Gods.
practical_application: Crisis medical care, restoration of system health, and mercy-based intervention.
academic_note: Specifically named as the best of healers among the deities.

name: Gefjun

sot_text.original: Fjórða er Gefjun. Hon er mær, ok henni þjóna þær er meyjar andast.

translations.faulkes_1987: The fourth is Gefjun. She is a virgin, and she is served by those who die as virgins.
translations.byock_2005: The fourth is Gefjun. She is a virgin, and those who die virgins serve her.
translations.brodeur_1916: The fourth is Gefjun: she is a virgin, and they that die maidens attend her.

historical_offerings: Agricultural symbols, plowshares, and dairy.
common_invocations: The Plow-Goddess, Protector of Virgins.
practical_application: Land surveyance, reclaiming lost resources, and foundational service.
academic_note: A virgin goddess served by those who die as virgins. Associated with plowing land from the sea.

name: Snotra

sot_text.original: Þrettánda Snotra. Hon er vitr ok fínlát.

translations.faulkes_1987: Thirteenth Snotra. She is wise and courtly.
translations.byock_2005: The thirteenth is Snotra. She is wise and courtly.
translations.brodeur_1916: The thirteenth is Snotra: she is prudent and courtly.

historical_offerings: Acts of hospitality and polite conduct.
common_invocations: The Courtly, The Wise Woman.
practical_application: Navigating complex social interfaces, maintaining self-control in heated debate, and hospitality.
academic_note: Prudent and courtly. Her name is derived from 'snotr' (wise/clever).

name: Bragi

sot_text.original: Einn heitir Bragi. Hann er ágætr at speki ok mest at málsnilli ok orðfimi. Hann kann mest af skáldskap.

translations.faulkes_1987: One is called Bragi. He is famous for wisdom and especially for eloquence and command of language. He knows most about poetry.
translations.byock_2005: One is named Bragi. He is famous for his wisdom and even more for his eloquence and command of language. He is a great master of poetry.
translations.brodeur_1916: One is called Bragi: he is renowned for wisdom, and most of all for fluency of speech and skill with words. He knows most of skaldship.

historical_offerings: Mealy wine, spoken poetry, and crafted songs.
common_invocations: The Long-Bearded, Master of Poetry.
practical_application: Public speaking, persuasive writing, and artistic skill.
academic_note: Famous for wisdom and eloquence. He knows most about poetry.

name: Höðr

sot_text.original: Höðr heitir einn Ássinn, hann er blindr. Œrit er hann styrkr, en vilja mundu goðin at þenna Áss þyrfti eigi at nefna.

translations.faulkes_1987: There is an Aesir called Hod. He is blind. He has sufficient strength, but the gods would have wished that there should be no need to mention this Aesir.
translations.byock_2005: There is an Aesir named Hod. He is blind. He is very strong, but the gods would have preferred that they had no reason to name this Aesir.
translations.brodeur_1916: Höðr is the name of one of the Æsir: he is blind. He is of sufficient strength, but the gods would desire that no occasion should rise of naming this Æsir.

historical_offerings: None recorded.
common_invocations: The Blind God, Slayer of Baldr.
practical_application: Acceptance of tragic fate, navigating blindness (physical or metaphorical), and handling immense strength.
academic_note: The blind god. Sufficiently strong, but associated with the tragedy of Baldr.

name: Víðarr

sot_text.original: Víðarr heitir einn, hinn þögli Áss. Hann á hvern skó þykkvan. Hann er djarfastr næst Þór.

translations.faulkes_1987: There is one called Vidar, the silent Aesir. He has a very thick shoe. He is the strongest next to Thor.
translations.byock_2005: One is named Vidar, the silent Aesir. He has a thick shoe. He is the strongest after Thor.
translations.brodeur_1916: Víðarr is the name of one, the silent Æsir. He has a thick shoe. He is nearly as strong as Thor.

historical_offerings: Leather scraps, meditative silence.
common_invocations: The Silent God, Avenger of Odin.
practical_application: Long-term endurance, avenging a systemic failure, and the strength of silence.
academic_note: The silent Aesir. Destined to avenge Odin at Ragnarök.

name: Váli

sot_text.original: Váli heitir einn, sonr Óðins ok Rindar. Hann er djarfr í orrostum ok mjök happskeytr.

translations.faulkes_1987: One is called Vali, son of Odin and Rind. He is daring in battle and a very good shot.
translations.byock_2005: One is named Vali, the son of Odin and Rind. He is daring in battle and an excellent marksman.
translations.brodeur_1916: Váli is the name of one, son of Odin and Rindr: he is daring in fights, and a most fortunate marksman.

historical_offerings: Archery targets, clear water, acts of loyalty.
common_invocations: The Avenger, The Marksman.
practical_application: Rapid response to injustice, focused survival, and rapid skill acquisition.
academic_note: Son of Odin and Rind. Daring in battle and a most fortunate marksman.

name: Ullr

sot_text.original: Ullr heitir einn, sonr Sifjar, stjúpsonr Þórs. Hann er bogmaðr svá góðr ok skíðfœrr svá at engi má við hann keppast.

translations.faulkes_1987: There is one called Ullr, son of Sif, stepson of Thor. He is such a good archer and skier that no one can compete with him.
translations.byock_2005: One is named Ullr, the son of Sif and the stepson of Thor. He is such a good archer and skier that no one can compete with him.
translations.brodeur_1916: Ullr is the name of one, son of Sif, stepson of Thor; he is so a good archer, and so able a skier that none may compete with him.

historical_offerings: Hunt trophies, ski-gear, sacred rings.
common_invocations: The Ski-God, Bowman of the Aesir.
practical_application: Navigating difficult terrain, hunting for resources, and fulfilling sacred oaths.
academic_note: Son of Sif. So good an archer and skier that none can compete with him.

name: Forseti

sot_text.original: Forseti heitir sonr Baldrs ok Nönnu Nepsdóttur. Hann á þann sal á himni er Glitnir heitir.

translations.faulkes_1987: Forseti is the name of the son of Baldr and Nanna Nepsdottir. He has a hall in heaven called Glitnir.
translations.byock_2005: Forseti is the name of the son of Baldr and Nanna, Nanna's daughter. He has a hall in heaven called Glitnir.
translations.brodeur_1916: Forseti is the name of the son of Baldr and Nanna, daughter of Nep: he has that hall in heaven which is called Glitnir.

historical_offerings: Agreements, written contracts, and peaceful dialogue.
common_invocations: The Arbitrator, Son of Baldr.
practical_application: Resolving long-standing disputes, mediation, and establishing legal fairness.
academic_note: Son of Baldr. He has the hall Glitnir and settles all disputes.

name: Sága

sot_text.original: Önnur er Sága. Hon býr á Sökkvabekk, ok er þat mikill bœr.

translations.faulkes_1987: The second is Saga. She lives at Sokkvabekk, and that is a large dwelling.
translations.byock_2005: The second is Saga. She lives at Sokkvabekk, and it is a large place.
translations.brodeur_1916: The second is Sága: she dwells at Sökkvabekkr, and very large is that abode.

historical_offerings: Golden cups, drink, and shared tales.
common_invocations: The Chronicler, Drinker with Odin.
practical_application: Archiving communal knowledge, preservation of heritage, and storytelling as a tool for prophecy.
academic_note: She dwells at Sökkvabekkr, and very large is that abode.

name: Fulla

sot_text.original: Fimmta er Fulla. Hon er enn mær ok gengr laushár ok hefir gullband um höfuð. Hon berr eski Friggjar.

translations.faulkes_1987: The fifth is Fulla. She is also a virgin and goes with her hair loose and has a golden band round her head. She carries Frigg's casket.
translations.byock_2005: The fifth is Fulla. She is also a virgin and goes with her hair flowing and a gold band around her head. She carries Frigg's jewelry box.
translations.brodeur_1916: The fifth is Fulla: she also is a maid, and goes with hair flowing and a golden band about her head; she bears Frigg's coffer.

historical_offerings: Golden bands, jewelry boxes, and shared secrets.
common_invocations: The Maid of Frigg, The Secret-Keeper.
practical_application: Maintaining security of internal communications, personal care, and protecting shared abundance.
academic_note: A maid who bears Frigg's coffer and goes with hair flowing.

name: Sjöfn

sot_text.original: Sjöunda er Sjöfn. Hon gætir mjök til at snúa hugum manna til ásta, kvenna ok karla.

translations.faulkes_1987: Seventh is Sjofn. She is much concerned to turn people's minds to love, both women and men.
translations.byock_2005: The seventh is Sjofn. She is much concerned with turning people's minds to love, both women and men.
translations.brodeur_1916: The seventh is Sjöfn: she is much concerned to turn men's minds to love, women and men alike.

historical_offerings: Social gifts, letters, and hospitality.
common_invocations: The Mind-Turner, Lady of Affection.
practical_application: Mending broken relationships, fostering communal affection, and emotional intelligence.
academic_note: Concerned to turn men's minds to love, women and men alike.

name: Lofn

sot_text.original: Átta er Lofn. Hon er svá mild ok góð til áheita at hon fær lof af Alföðr eða Frigg til samgangs manna.

translations.faulkes_1987: Eighth is Lofn. She is so kind and good to pray to that she gets permission from All-father or Frigg for people to come together in marriage.
translations.byock_2005: The eighth is Lofn. She is so kind and good to pray to that she gets permission from All-father or Frigg for people to come together.
translations.brodeur_1916: The eighth is Lofn: she is so gracious and kindly to pray to, that she wins leave from the All-father or Frigg for the coming together of mankind.

historical_offerings: Vows of loyalty and permission-seeking prayers.
common_invocations: The Gracious, Permitter of Marriage.
practical_application: Gaining permission for a difficult union, mediating between authority and individual desire.
academic_note: So gracious that she wins leave from Frigg for the coming together of mankind.

name: Vár

sot_text.original: Níunda er Vár. Hon hlýðir á eiða manna ok einkamál er konur ok karlar gera sín á milli.

translations.faulkes_1987: Ninth is Var. She listens to people's oaths and private agreements that women and men make between each other.
translations.byock_2005: The ninth is Var. She listens to people's oaths and the private agreements that women and men make between themselves.
translations.brodeur_1916: The ninth is Vár: she hearkens to the oaths of men and the private compacts which women and men make between each other.

historical_offerings: Solemn oaths, written contracts, and shared drinking.
common_invocations: The Listener of Oaths, Goddess of Vows.
practical_application: Verifying the truth of a contract, establishing an unbreakable vow, and notarizing agreements.
academic_note: She hearkens to the oaths of men and private compacts.

name: Vör

sot_text.original: Tíunda er Vör. Hon er ok vitr ok spurul svá at ekki má hana leyna.

translations.faulkes_1987: Tenth is Vor. She is also wise and searching so that nothing can be concealed from her.
translations.byock_2005: The tenth is Vor. She is also wise and inquiring, so that nothing can be hidden from her.
translations.brodeur_1916: The tenth is Vör: she is wise and of searching spirit, so that nothing can be hidden from her.

historical_offerings: Meditative focus and investigative work.
common_invocations: The Wise, The Searching One.
practical_application: Uncovering hidden data, intuition-led investigation, and deep-dive research.
academic_note: Wise and searching; nothing can be hidden from her.

name: Syn

sot_text.original: Ellifta er Syn. Hon gætir dura í höllinni ok lýkr fyrir þeim er eigi skulu inn ganga.

translations.faulkes_1987: Eleventh is Syn. She guards the doors in the hall and shuts them against those who are not to enter.
translations.byock_2005: The eleventh is Syn. She guards the doors of the hall and shuts them against those who are not supposed to enter.
translations.brodeur_1916: The eleventh is Syn: she keeps the door in the hall, and locks it before those who should not go in.

historical_offerings: Locked doors, boundary markers, legal arguments.
common_invocations: Guard of the Doors, The Refuser.
practical_application: Establishing secure firewalls, saying 'No' to encroachment, and boundary defense.
academic_note: She keeps the door in the hall and locks it before those who should not go in.

name: Hlín

sot_text.original: Tólfta er Hlín. Hon er sett til gæzlu yfir þeim mönnum er Frigg vill forða við háska nökkurum.

translations.faulkes_1987: Twelfth is Hlin. She is set to guard those people whom Frigg wishes to save from some danger.
translations.byock_2005: The twelfth is Hlin. She is appointed to guard those people whom Frigg wants to protect from some danger.
translations.brodeur_1916: The twelfth is Hlín: she is established as keeper over those men whom Frigg desires to preserve from any danger.

historical_offerings: Acts of rescue, safety measures, quiet care.
common_invocations: The Protector, Guard of the Endangered.
practical_application: Disaster recovery, providing refuge for those in danger, and consolation.
academic_note: Established as keeper over those men whom Frigg desires to preserve from danger.

name: Gná

sot_text.original: Fjórtánda er Gná. Hana sendir Frigg í ýmsa heima at sínum erindum.

translations.faulkes_1987: Fourteenth is Gna. Frigg sends her into various worlds on her business.
translations.byock_2005: The fourteenth is Gna. Frigg sends her into various worlds on her business.
translations.brodeur_1916: The fourteenth is Gná: her Frigg sends into many lands on her errands.

historical_offerings: Hoof-prints, courier items, swift action.
common_invocations: The Messenger, Rider of Hofvarpnir.
practical_application: Rapid delivery of information, physical speed, and cross-boundary communication.
academic_note: Frigg sends her into many lands on her errands.

name: Sif

sot_text.original: Sif er kona Þórs. Hon er fögr ok hefir gullit hár.

translations.faulkes_1987: Sif is Thor's wife. She is beautiful and has golden hair.
translations.byock_2005: Sif is Thor's wife. She is fair and has golden hair.
translations.brodeur_1916: Sif is Thor's wife: she is fair and has golden hair.

historical_offerings: Golden grain, corn-dollies, metalwork.
common_invocations: The Golden-Haired, Wife of Thor.
practical_application: Ensuring the success of a long-term harvest, marital fidelity, and material wealth from the earth.
academic_note: Thor's wife. Fair and has golden hair, representing the grain of the earth.

name: Iðunn

sot_text.original: Iðunn geymir í eski sínu epli þau er goðin skulu á bíta er þau eldast, ok verða þá allir ungir.

translations.faulkes_1987: Idunn keeps in her casket those apples which the gods must taste when they grow old, and then they all become young again.
translations.byock_2005: Idunn keeps in her box the apples which the gods must eat when they grow old, and they all then become young again.
translations.brodeur_1916: Idunn keeps in her chest those apples which the gods must taste when they grow old; and then they all become young again.

historical_offerings: Apples, nuts, mead.
common_invocations: The Renewer, Keeper of the Apples.
practical_application: Health maintenance, youthfulness of spirit, and the renovation of a system.
academic_note: Idunn keeps in her casket those apples which the gods must taste to stay young.

name: Nanna

sot_text.original: Nanna Nepsdóttur var kona Baldrs. Hon sprac af harmi ok dó.

translations.faulkes_1987: Nanna Nepsdottir was Baldr's wife. She died of a broken heart.
translations.byock_2005: Nanna Nepsdottir was Baldr's wife. She burst from grief and died.
translations.brodeur_1916: Nanna Nepsdóttir was Baldr's wife. She died of a broken heart.

historical_offerings: Memorial gifts, tokens of love.
common_invocations: The Devoted, Wife of Baldr.
practical_application: Devotion to a partner, processing grief, and finding peace through tragedy.
academic_note: Baldr's wife. She died of a broken heart and was burned on his funeral pyre.

name: Hermóðr

sot_text.original: Hermóðr er kallaðr inn hvati, sonr Óðins.

translations.faulkes_1987: Hermod is called the nimble, son of Odin.
translations.byock_2005: Hermod is named the bold, the son of Odin.
translations.brodeur_1916: Hermóðr the Bold is he called, Odin's son.

historical_offerings: Message tokens, items of travel.
common_invocations: The Bold, The Nimble Messenger.
practical_application: High-stakes communication, daring physical action, and swift diplomatic intervention.
academic_note: Odin's son, called the nimble. He rode Sleipnir into Hel to treat for Baldr.

name: Hœnir

sot_text.original: Hœnir, er kallaðr var hinn skjálgi eða inn þögli Áss.

translations.faulkes_1987: Hoenir, who was called the long-legged or the silent Aesir.
translations.byock_2005: Hoenir, who is called the long-legged or the silent Aesir.
translations.brodeur_1916: Hœnir, who is called the long-legged or the silent Æsir.

historical_offerings: Symbolic representations of the spark of life.
common_invocations: The Silent God, The Long-Legged.
practical_application: Finding the 'spirit' of a project, silent creation, and overcoming indecision through distance.
academic_note: One of the three primordial gods involved in the creation of mankind.
